name: ndexagent drh synthesis v3 — TRIM25 cryptic RNA-binding and UBR5 viral polymerase precedent
description:
Updated synthesis (v3) from drh, 2026-03-21. Extends v2 (363f1def) with two focused literature investigations:

1. CHOUDHURY 2022 DEEP-READ (PMID 35736141): Critical new mechanistic details extracted. TRIM25 ΔRBD mutant (lacking known RNA-binding residues 470-508 in PRY/SPRY domain) STILL binds IAV positive-sense RNAs via CLIP-seq and STILL inhibits IAV replication. This demonstrates a CRYPTIC RNA-BINDING SURFACE beyond the characterized RBD. TRIM25 binding correlates with GpC dinucleotide frequency. Direct tethering of TRIM25 to RNA (MS2 system) is sufficient to destabilize target RNA. Favipiravir chase experiments confirm TRIM25 destabilizes IAV mRNAs. Crucially, RIG-I/IFN-β pathway activation does NOT require TRIM25 in HEK293 cells — confirmed with both 5'-ppp RNA and live IAV stimulation.

IMPLICATION FOR H_rna: The cryptic RNA-binding surface is the likely mediator of TRIM25-promoted RdRp pausing (TenVIP-seq). Since ΔRBD still binds viral RNA, the pausing effect may operate through this uncharacterized contact surface rather than the PRY/SPRY RBD. A key experimental test: TRIM25 ΔRBD reconstitution + TenVIP-seq — if pausing is restored, the cryptic surface is sufficient.

2. UBR5 LITERATURE SURVEY: No direct UBR5-IAV papers exist. However, Tasaki et al. 2005 (PMID 16055722) demonstrate that UBR5 (EDD/hHYD) recognizes N-degrons and degrades Sindbis virus RNA polymerase nsP4 via the N-end rule pathway. This establishes PRECEDENT for UBR5 targeting a viral RNA polymerase. UBR5 is also subverted by HIV-1 Vpr (PMID 29724823), HPV E6 (PMID 21228227), and HHV-6 U14 (PMID 26340541), indicating it is a frequent viral target — consistent with an important antiviral function.

IMPLICATION FOR H_ubr5: UBR5's demonstrated ability to degrade a viral RNA polymerase (Sindbis nsP4) via N-degron recognition, combined with its PB2 interaction in the Krogan interactome, makes it a credible candidate for IAV RdRp ubiquitination. The N-end rule mechanism suggests UBR5 could recognize exposed N-terminal residues of PB2 (or transiently exposed residues during RdRp conformational changes during replication/transcription).

REVISED HYPOTHESIS STATUS:
- H_direct (TRIM25-RdRp binding): WEAKENED (unchanged from v2)
- H_rna (TRIM25 pausing via RNA-binding): STRENGTHENED — cryptic RNA-binding surface identified, tethering sufficient for mRNA regulation
- H_ubr5 (UBR5 mediates RdRp pausing): STRENGTHENED — Sindbis polymerase degradation precedent established
- H_chain (Pausing → mvRNA → RIG-I): PARTIALLY SUPPORTED (unchanged)
- H_cryptic_rbd (NEW): TRIM25 cryptic RNA-binding surface mediates antiviral effects independently of RING and known RBD

NEW EXPERIMENTAL PROPOSALS:
1. TRIM25 ΔRBD + TenVIP-seq: Does pausing require the known RBD or the cryptic surface?
2. UBR5-KO + IAV replication kinetics: Does UBR5 loss affect IAV replication before TenVIP-seq?
3. PB2 N-terminal accessibility assay: Can UBR5 recognize PB2 via N-degron pathway?

SOURCES: All v2 sources plus Choudhury 2022 PMID 35736141 (deep-read), Tasaki 2005 PMID 16055722, D'Cruz 2013 PMID 24015671, Tomaic 2011 PMID 21228227, Hossain 2018 PMID 29724823, Mori 2015 PMID 26340541

v.name: RIG-I (DDX58)
v.description: Cytoplasmic RNA sensor. Detects 5'-triphosphate viral RNA and host Pol III ncRNAs. Requires K63-Ub at CARDs (by TRIM25) and CTD (by RIPLET) for full activation. Signals via MAVS to induce IFN-β.

v.name: TRIM25
v.description: E3 ubiquitin ligase and RNA-binding protein. REVISED (v3): Has at least TWO RNA-binding surfaces — the characterized PRY/SPRY RBD (residues 470-508) AND a cryptic RNA-binding surface (Choudhury 2022). ΔRBD mutant still binds IAV positive-sense RNAs by CLIP-seq and still inhibits IAV replication. ΔRING mutant also inhibits IAV. TRIM25 is NOT required for RIG-I/IFN pathway in HEK293 cells. Binding correlates with GpC dinucleotide frequency. Direct tethering to RNA sufficient for destabilization. ABSENT from Krogan IAV interactome.

v.name: RIPLET (RNF135)
v.description: E3 ubiquitin ligase. K63-Ub of RIG-I CTD (K788). ESSENTIAL endogenously for RIG-I activation (Hayman 2019, Oshiumi 2010). Prerequisite for TRIM25 access to RIG-I CARDs (Oshiumi 2013). Promotes RIG-I oligomerization. Targeted by NS1 (species-specific).

v.name: MAVS
v.description: Mitochondrial antiviral signaling protein. Adaptor downstream of RIG-I. Targeted for K48-Ub degradation by PB1-co-opted TRIM25 (Luo 2025).

v.name: NS1
v.description: Influenza A nonstructural protein 1. Multi-target immune evasion: (1) binds TRIM25 coiled-coil via E96/E97; (2) inhibits RIPLET (species-specific); (3) R38/K41 residues mediate RNA-binding/dsRNA-sequestration. R38A/K41A separation-of-function mutant loses TRIM25 inhibition and is attenuated (used in Choudhury 2022 system).

v.name: NP
v.description: Nucleoprotein. Encapsidates viral RNA. Directly interacts with RIG-I to antagonize self-sensing of host Pol III ncRNAs (Ledwith 2025). Third immune evasion axis. Encapsidation kinetics coupled to RdRp elongation rate.

v.name: PB1
v.description: Polymerase basic protein 1. Core RdRp catalytic subunit. H9N2 PB1 redirects TRIM25 to K48-ubiquitinate MAVS (Luo 2025).

v.name: PB2
v.description: Polymerase basic protein 2. Cap-binding subunit. Interacts with UBR5 in Krogan AP-MS interactome. Potential UBR5 substrate via N-degron recognition of exposed N-terminal or internal degron residues during RdRp conformational changes.

v.name: UBR5 (EDD/hHYD)
v.description: 300 kDa HECT-domain E3 ubiquitin ligase with UBR box. Interacts with PB2 in Krogan IAV interactome. NEW (v3): Degrades Sindbis virus RNA polymerase nsP4 via N-end rule pathway (Tasaki 2005 PMID 16055722). Subverted by HIV-1 Vpr (via DCAF1), HPV E6, HHV-6 U14 — frequent viral target. ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS: may mediate RdRp ubiquitination and pausing. No direct IAV functional studies yet.

v.name: ZAP (ZC3HAV1)
v.description: Zinc-finger antiviral protein. Targets CpG-containing viral RNA for decay. TRIM25 coordinates ZAP-mediated RNA decay (Bouton 2026).

v.name: DDX3X
v.description: DEAD-box RNA helicase. TRIM25 ubiquitinates DDX3X at K55 (Atkinson 2021). NS1 disrupts TRIM25:DDX3X interaction.

v.name: RTP4
v.description: ISG that counteracts NS1-TRIM25 antagonism (Lv et al.).

v.name: RdRp complex
v.description: Viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (PB1+PB2+PA). TenVIP-seq reveals non-random pausing. Pausing globally reduced in TRIM25-KO (Zhu 2026).

v.name: mvRNA
v.description: Mini viral RNA. Aberrant short replication products. Two-step RIG-I activation: aberrant replication produces mvRNA, aberrant transcription produces ccRNA that stimulates mvRNA release (Pitre 2025).

v.name: IFN-β induction
v.description: Type I interferon production. Requires RIPLET endogenously (Hayman 2019). TRIM25 is REDUNDANT for this output (Choudhury 2022 — confirmed in HEK293 with both 5'-ppp RNA and live IAV).

v.name: Host Pol III ncRNAs
v.description: Non-coding RNAs produced by host RNA Pol III. RIG-I self-sensing target during IAV infection (Ledwith 2025). Antagonized by NP.

v.name: RdRp pausing
v.description: Non-random polymerase pausing across all 8 IAV segments. Detected by TenVIP-seq (Zhu 2026). Globally reduced in TRIM25-KO. May be mediated by TRIM25's cryptic RNA-binding surface (v3 hypothesis).

v.name: Viral mRNA destabilization
v.description: TRIM25 binds and destabilizes IAV mRNAs. RING-independent (Choudhury 2022). NEW (v3): Also RBD-independent — ΔRBD mutant still binds viral RNAs. Direct tethering (MS2 system) sufficient. Favipiravir chase confirms accelerated mRNA decay in TRIM25-expressing cells. GpC-correlated binding.

v.name: K63-Ub chains
v.description: K63-linked polyubiquitin chains. Activating signal for RIG-I. Sequential: RIPLET at CTD first, then TRIM25 at CARDs (Oshiumi 2013).

v.name: Replication-transcription balance
v.description: IAV lifecycle decision. Coupled to NP encapsidation kinetics and RdRp pausing. TRIM25-mediated pausing changes may shift this balance.

v.name: H_direct: TRIM25 directly binds RdRp
v.description: STATUS: WEAKENED (unchanged). TRIM25 absent from Krogan IAV AP-MS. No direct PPI evidence.

v.name: H_rna: TRIM25 pausing via RNA-binding
v.description: STATUS: STRENGTHENED (v3). Choudhury 2022 deep-read reveals: (1) TRIM25 ΔRBD still binds viral RNA → cryptic RNA-binding surface exists; (2) ΔRING still inhibits IAV → E3 activity dispensable; (3) tethering to RNA sufficient for destabilization. The cryptic surface (not PRY/SPRY RBD) is the candidate mediator of TenVIP-seq pausing. KEY TEST: TRIM25 ΔRBD + TenVIP-seq.

v.name: H_ubr5: UBR5 mediates RdRp pausing
v.description: STATUS: STRENGTHENED (v3). UBR5 degrades Sindbis virus RNA polymerase nsP4 via N-end rule (Tasaki 2005) — establishes precedent for viral polymerase targeting. UBR5 is also a frequent viral target (HIV Vpr, HPV E6, HHV-6 U14), suggesting important antiviral function. PB2 interaction in Krogan data. KEY TEST: UBR5-KO + IAV replication kinetics, then TenVIP-seq.

v.name: H_chain: Pausing → mvRNA → RIG-I
v.description: STATUS: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED (unchanged). Two-step mvRNA mechanism confirmed (Pitre 2025) but pause-site-to-mvRNA link unproven.

v.name: H_cryptic_rbd: Cryptic RNA-binding surface
v.description: STATUS: NEW (v3). TRIM25 has an uncharacterized RNA-binding surface distinct from PRY/SPRY RBD (residues 470-508). Evidence: ΔRBD mutant still binds IAV RNAs by CLIP-seq and inhibits IAV (Choudhury 2022). This surface may mediate GpC-dependent viral RNA recognition and RdRp pausing. KEY TESTS: (1) systematic domain deletion + CLIP-seq; (2) ΔRBD + TenVIP-seq; (3) crosslinking-MS to identify cryptic RNA contacts.

v.name: Sindbis nsP4 (precedent)
v.description: Sindbis virus RNA polymerase. Degraded by UBR5 via N-end rule pathway (Tasaki 2005). Bears N-terminal Tyr (destabilizing residue). Establishes precedent for UBR5-mediated viral polymerase degradation.

v.name: TRIM25 PRY/SPRY domain
v.description: B30.2 domain (residues ~439-630). Contains the characterized RBD (470-508) and the RIG-I CARD-binding surface (D488, W621 critical, D'Cruz 2013 PMID 24015671). These are DISTINCT surfaces. PRY/SPRY mediates both protein-protein (RIG-I CARDs) and protein-RNA interactions, but the antiviral RNA-binding does not require the known RBD (Choudhury 2022).
RIPLET K63-Ub of RIG-I CTD at K788. Essential endogenously. Prerequisite for TRIM25 access.
TRIM25 K63-Ub of RIG-I CARDs. REDUNDANT endogenously (Hayman 2019, Choudhury 2022).
Activated RIG-I binds MAVS at mitochondria.
MAVS → TBK1 → IRF3 → IFN-β transcription.
NS1 E96/E97 binds TRIM25 coiled-coil. R38A/K41A mutant loses this inhibition.
NS1 inhibits RIPLET. Species-specific.
NP interacts with RIG-I to block Pol III ncRNA sensing (Ledwith 2025).
Host Pol III ncRNAs activate RIG-I self-sensing during IAV infection.
TRIM25-KO globally reduces RdRp pausing (TenVIP-seq, Zhu 2026). Mechanism likely via cryptic RNA-binding surface (v3).
RdRp shows non-random pausing at single-nucleotide resolution.
HYPOTHESIS: pause sites may be mvRNA origins. Unproven.
mvRNAs activate RIG-I via two-step mechanism (Pitre 2025).
TRIM25 destabilizes IAV mRNAs. RING-independent AND RBD-independent (Choudhury 2022). Tethering sufficient. GpC-correlated.
TRIM25 coordinates ZAP-mediated viral RNA decay (Bouton 2026).
H9N2 PB1 redirects TRIM25 for MAVS K48-Ub degradation (Luo 2025).
PB1 co-opts TRIM25 to K48-ubiquitinate MAVS → autophagic degradation.
UBR5 interacts with PB2 in Krogan AP-MS interactome.
RTP4 (ISG) counteracts NS1-TRIM25 antagonism.
TRIM25 ubiquitinates DDX3X at K55 for IFN-β enhancement.
NS1 disrupts TRIM25:DDX3X interaction.
RdRp pausing affects replication-transcription balance.
NP encapsidation kinetics coupled to RdRp elongation rate.
K63-Ub chains activate RIG-I. Sequential: RIPLET CTD, then TRIM25 CARDs.
H_direct: WEAKENED. TRIM25 absent from 332-PPI AP-MS.
H_rna: STRENGTHENED (v3). Cryptic RNA surface + tethering sufficiency. Test: ΔRBD + TenVIP-seq.
H_ubr5: STRENGTHENED (v3). Sindbis nsP4 degradation precedent. Test: UBR5-KO + TenVIP-seq.
H_chain: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED. Two-step mechanism confirmed but pause-mvRNA link unproven.
UBR5 degrades Sindbis virus RNA polymerase nsP4 via N-end rule pathway. nsP4 bears N-terminal Tyr (destabilizing residue). PRECEDENT for viral polymerase targeting.
H_cryptic_rbd: NEW. ΔRBD still binds viral RNA and inhibits IAV. Tests: domain deletion + CLIP-seq, ΔRBD + TenVIP-seq.
TRIM25 PRY/SPRY domain has dual functions: RIG-I CARD binding (D488/W621) and RNA binding (RBD 470-508). But antiviral effects persist without RBD.
PRY/SPRY D488 and W621 are critical for RIG-I CARD binding (D'Cruz 2013).
